children.heading: General authority of the Secretary
children.label: a General authority of the Secretary

children.heading: Determination of claims
children.label: 1 Determination of claims
children.text: Except in the case of an at-risk amount required under or the payment or disposition of a State share under , the Secretary shall have the power to determine the amount of and settle and adjust any claim and to compromise or deny all or part of any such claim or claims arising under the provisions of this chapter or the regulations issued pursuant to this chapter, including, but not limited to, claims arising from fraudulent and nonfraudulent overissuances to recipients, including the power to waive claims if the Secretary determines that to do so would serve the purposes of this chapter. Such powers with respect to claims against recipients may be delegated by the Secretary to State agencies. The Secretary shall have the power to reduce amounts otherwise due to a State agency under to collect unpaid claims assessed against the State agency if the State agency has declined or exhausted its appeal rights under .section 2025(c)(1)(D)(i)(II) of this titlesection 2013(a)(2) of this titlesection 2025 of this titlesection 2023 of this title

children.heading: Claims established under quality control system
children.label: 2 Claims established under quality control system
children.text: To the extent that a State agency does not pay a claim established under , including an agreement to have all or part of the claim paid through a reduction in Federal administrative funding, within 30 days from the date on which the bill for collection is received by the State agency, the State agency shall be liable for interest on any unpaid portion of such claim accruing from the date on which the bill for collection was received by the State agency, unless the State agency appeals the claim under . If the State agency appeals such claim (in whole or in part), the interest on any unpaid portion of the claim shall accrue from the date of the decision on the administrative appeal, or from a date that is 1 year after the date the bill is received, whichever is earlier, until the date the unpaid portion of the payment is received. If the State agency pays such claim (in whole or in part, including an agreement to have all or part of the claim paid through a reduction in Federal administrative funding) and the claim is subsequently overturned through administrative or judicial appeal, any amounts paid by the State agency shall be promptly returned with interest, accruing from the date the payment is received until the date the payment is returned.section 2025(c)(1) of this titlesection 2025(c)(7) of this title

children.heading: Computation of interest
children.label: 3 Computation of interest
children.text: Any interest assessed under this paragraph shall be computed at a rate determined by the Secretary based on the average of the bond equivalent of the weekly 90-day Treasury bill auction rates during the period such interest accrues.

children.heading: Joint and several liability of household members
children.label: 4 Joint and several liability of household members
children.text: Each adult member of a household shall be jointly and severally liable for the value of any overissuance of benefits.

children.label: A
children.text: reducing the allotment of the household;

children.label: B
children.text: withholding amounts from unemployment compensation from a member of the household under subsection (c);

children.label: C
children.text: recovering from Federal pay or a Federal income tax refund under subsection (d); or

children.label: D
children.text: any other means.

children.heading: Cost effectiveness
children.label: 2 Cost effectiveness
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall not apply if the State agency demonstrates to the satisfaction of the Secretary that all of the means referred to in paragraph (1) are not cost effective.

children.label: A
children.text: 10 percent of the monthly allotment of the household; or

children.heading: Procedures
children.label: 4 Procedures
children.text: A State agency shall collect an overissuance of benefits issued to a household under paragraph (1) in accordance with the requirements established by the State agency for providing notice, electing a means of payment, and establishing a time schedule for payment.

children.heading: Overissuances caused by systemic State errors
children.label: 5 Overissuances caused by systemic State errors

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If the Secretary determines that a State agency overissued benefits to a substantial number of households in a fiscal year as a result of a major systemic error by the State agency, as defined by the Secretary, the Secretary may prohibit the State agency from collecting these overissuances from some or all households.

children.heading: Information reporting by States
children.label: i Information reporting by States
children.text: Every State agency shall provide to the Secretary all information requested by the Secretary concerning the issuance of benefits to households by the State agency in the applicable fiscal year.

children.label: I
children.text: whether the State agency overissued benefits to a substantial number of households as a result of a systemic error in the applicable fiscal year; and

children.label: II
children.text: as to the amount of the overissuance in the applicable fiscal year for which the State agency is liable.

children.heading: Establishing a claim
children.label: iii Establishing a claim
children.text: Upon determining under clause (ii) that a State agency has overissued benefits to households due to a major systemic error determined under subparagraph (A), the Secretary shall establish a claim against the State agency equal to the value of the overissuance caused by the systemic error.

children.heading: Administrative and judicial review
children.label: iv Administrative and judicial review
children.text: Administrative and judicial review, as provided in , shall apply to the final determinations by the Secretary under clause (ii).section 2023 of this title

children.heading: Determination not appealed
children.label: I Determination not appealed
children.text: If the determination of the Secretary under clause (ii) is not appealed, the State agency shall, as soon as practicable, remit to the Secretary the dollar amount specified in the claim under clause (iii).

children.heading: Determination appealed
children.label: II Determination appealed
children.text: If the determination of the Secretary under clause (ii) is appealed, upon completion of administrative and judicial review under clause (iv), and a finding of liability on the part of the State, the appealing State agency shall, as soon as practicable, remit to the Secretary a dollar amount subject to the finding made in the administrative and judicial review.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: If a State agency fails to make a payment under clause (v) within a reasonable period of time, as determined by the Secretary, the Secretary may reduce any amount due to the State agency under any other provision of this chapter by the amount due.

children.heading: Accrual of interest
children.label: II Accrual of interest
children.text: During the period of time determined by the Secretary to be reasonable under subclause (I), interest in the amount owed shall not accrue.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: vii Limitation
children.text: Any liability amount established under shall be reduced by the amount of the claim established under this subparagraph.section 2025(c)(1)(C) of this title

As used in this subsection, the term “uncollected overissuance” means the amount of an overissuance of benefits, as determined under subsection (b)(1), that has not been recovered pursuant to subsection (b)(1).

A State agency may determine on a periodic basis, from information supplied pursuant to , whether an individual receiving compensation under the State’s unemployment compensation law (including amounts payable pursuant to an agreement under a Federal unemployment compensation law) owes an uncollected overissuance.section 49b(b) of title 29

children.label: i
children.text: entering into an agreement with an individual described in paragraph (2) under which specified amounts will be withheld from unemployment compensation otherwise payable to the individual; and

children.label: ii
children.text: furnishing a copy of the agreement to the State agency administering the unemployment compensation law; or

children.label: B
children.text: in the absence of an agreement, by obtaining a writ, order, summons, or other similar process in the nature of garnishment from a court of competent jurisdiction to require the withholding of amounts from the unemployment compensation.

children.heading: Recovery of overissuance of benefits
children.label: d Recovery of overissuance of benefits
children.text: The amount of an overissuance of benefits, as determined under subsection (b)(1), that has not been recovered pursuant to such subsection may be recovered from Federal pay (including salaries and pensions) as authorized by or a Federal income tax refund as authorized by .section 5514 of title 5section 3720A of title 31
